pattern: Sweeping left to right while tracking the running maximum of the chosen left part and the maximum seen so far lets a single pass find the shortest prefix whose every element bounds the rest.
hints: The left part is valid only if its largest element never exceeds the smallest element to its right.
Track the maximum of the committed left part and a separate running maximum of everything scanned.
Whenever an element is smaller than the left part's maximum, the boundary must extend to here, so move the cut point and raise the left maximum to the running maximum.

edge_inputs.description: a dip after a high prefix forces the cut to include the dip
trailing small value pulls the boundary rightward
strictly increasing array cuts after the first element
all equal values still split after one element
large early peak forces nearly the whole array left
late minimum after a flat run extends the boundary

stress.description: construct a guaranteed valid partition by joining a bounded left block to a larger right block
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    cut = rng.randint(1, n - 1)
    left = [rng.randrange(0, 500) for _ in range(cut)]
    hi = max(left)
    right = [rng.randrange(hi + 1, hi + 600) for _ in range(n - cut)]
    return [left + right]
stress.note: every left element is strictly below every right element, so a valid answer always exists
